skill.name: webapp-testing
skill.description: Toolkit for testing local web applications with Playwright. It helps verify frontend behavior, inspect rendered pages, capture screenshots, and collect browser console logs.
skill.summary: Test and debug local web applications with Playwright browser automation.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: Official Anthropic skill reviewed as safe for marketplace publication. Static flags reflect expected local browser testing behavior: localhost access, output file creation, and helper script command execution.

content.value_statement: Local web application testing often needs reliable browser automation, screenshots, and console diagnostics. This skill guides Playwright scripts that inspect rendered pages, manage local servers, and verify UI behavior.
actual_capabilities: Guides Python Playwright scripts for local browser automation.
Starts one or more local servers before running a test command.
Captures screenshots for visual inspection of rendered pages.
Discovers buttons, links, and form inputs from the live DOM.
Collects browser console messages during interaction flows.
Supports static HTML testing through local file URLs.
limitations: Requires Playwright and browser dependencies in the execution environment.
Focuses on local applications, not remote production monitoring.
Generated scripts still need selectors that match the target application.
The helper runs user supplied server commands, so commands must be trusted.

use_cases.title: Verify a frontend change
use_cases.description: Run a local app, click through the changed UI, and capture screenshots for review.

use_cases.title: Debug browser behavior
use_cases.description: Inspect rendered elements and collect console messages when a page behaves differently than expected.

use_cases.title: Test static prototypes
use_cases.description: Open local HTML files with Playwright and verify forms, buttons, and visual states.

prompt_templates.title: Open a local page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check of a running development server.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the webapp-testing skill to open my local app at http://localhost:5173, wait for it to load, and take a screenshot.

prompt_templates.title: Find interactive elements
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the webapp-testing skill to inspect the rendered page and list visible buttons, links, inputs, and useful selectors.

prompt_templates.title: Run with a managed server
prompt_templates.scenario: Start a development server and run browser checks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the webapp-testing skill to start my app with npm run dev on port 5173, then verify the main navigation and form submission flow.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose a UI failure
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced debugging with screenshots and console logs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the webapp-testing skill to reproduce this UI issue, capture console logs, save before and after screenshots, and summarize likely causes.

output_examples.input: Check whether the dashboard page loads and shows the expected controls.
output: Opened the local dashboard after the server became ready.
Found the main navigation, search input, and export button.
Saved a full page screenshot for visual review.

output_examples.input: Debug why clicking the profile form submit button appears to do nothing.
output: Captured console messages during the form interaction.
Confirmed the submit button was visible and clickable.
Observed a validation message that appears only after the network idle wait.

output_examples.input: Inspect a static HTML prototype and verify the contact form path.
output: Opened the local HTML file through a file URL.
Filled the name and email fields.
Captured the final screen after submitting the form.

best_practices: Wait for network idle before inspecting dynamic pages.
Use stable role, text, ID, or CSS selectors for interactions.
Close browsers and stop managed servers after each run.
anti_patterns: Testing a dynamic app before JavaScript has finished rendering.
Using fragile selectors that depend on incidental layout details.
Running untrusted server commands through the helper script.

faq.question: Does this skill require Playwright?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides Python Playwright scripts and assumes the needed browser dependencies are available.

faq.question: Can it start my development server?
faq.answer: Yes. The included helper can start one or more local servers, wait for ports, run a command, and clean up.

faq.question: Can it test static HTML files?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples show how to open local HTML files with file URLs and interact with page elements.

faq.question: Can it capture screenshots?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports full page screenshots for rendered web pages and static HTML prototypes.

faq.question: Does it collect browser console logs?
faq.answer: Yes. The console logging example records browser messages during page navigation and user interactions.

faq.question: Is it intended for production monitoring?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for local development testing and debugging workflows.
